--------------------------------------------------
>> Issue: [B107:hardcoded_password_default] Possible hardcoded password: 'upgrades'
   Severity: Low   Confidence: Medium
   CWE: CWE-259 (https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/259.html)
   More Info: https://bandit.readthedocs.io/en/1.9.4/plugins/b107_hardcoded_password_default.html
   Location: .\.venv\Lib\site-packages\alembic\runtime\environment.py:414:4
413	
414	    def configure(
415	        self,
416	        connection: Optional[Connection] = None,
417	        url: Optional[Union[str, URL]] = None,
418	        dialect_name: Optional[str] = None,
419	        dialect_opts: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
420	        transactional_ddl: Optional[bool] = None,
421	        transaction_per_migration: bool = False,
422	        output_buffer: Optional[TextIO] = None,
423	        starting_rev: Optional[str] = None,
424	        tag: Optional[str] = None,
425	        template_args: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
426	        render_as_batch: bool = False,
427	        target_metadata: Union[MetaData, Sequence[MetaData], None] = None,
428	        include_name: Optional[IncludeNameFn] = None,
429	        include_object: Optional[IncludeObjectFn] = None,
430	        include_schemas: bool = False,
431	        process_revision_directives: Optional[
432	            ProcessRevisionDirectiveFn
433	        ] = None,
434	        compare_type: Union[bool, CompareType] = True,
435	        compare_server_default: Union[bool, CompareServerDefault] = False,
436	        render_item: Optional[RenderItemFn] = None,
437	        literal_binds: bool = False,
438	        upgrade_token: str = "upgrades",
439	        downgrade_token: str = "downgrades",
440	        alembic_module_prefix: str = "op.",
441	        sqlalchemy_module_prefix: str = "sa.",
442	        user_module_prefix: Optional[str] = None,
443	        on_version_apply: Optional[OnVersionApplyFn] = None,
444	        autogenerate_plugins: Sequence[str] | None = None,
445	        **kw: Any,
446	    ) -> None:
447	        """Configure a :class:`.MigrationContext` within this
448	        :class:`.EnvironmentContext` which will provide database
449	        connectivity and other configuration to a series of
450	        migration scripts.
451	
452	        Many methods on :class:`.EnvironmentContext` require that
453	        this method has been called in order to function, as they
454	        ultimately need to have database access or at least access
455	        to the dialect in use.  Those which do are documented as such.
456	
457	        The important thing needed by :meth:`.configure` is a
458	        means to determine what kind of database dialect is in use.
459	        An actual connection to that database is needed only if
460	        the :class:`.MigrationContext` is to be used in
461	        "online" mode.
462	
463	        If the :meth:`.is_offline_mode` function returns ``True``,
464	        then no connection is needed here.  Otherwise, the
465	        ``connection`` parameter should be present as an
466	        instance of :class:`sqlalchemy.engine.Connection`.
467	
468	        This function is typically called from the ``env.py``
469	        script within a migration environment.  It can be called
470	        multiple times for an invocation.  The most recent
471	        :class:`~sqlalchemy.engine.Connection`
472	        for which it was called is the one that will be operated upon
473	        by the next call to :meth:`.run_migrations`.
474	
475	        General parameters:
476	
477	        :param connection: a :class:`~sqlalchemy.engine.Connection`
478	         to use
479	         for SQL execution in "online" mode.  When present, is also
480	         used to determine the type of dialect in use.
481	        :param url: a string database url, or a
482	         :class:`sqlalchemy.engine.url.URL` object.
483	         The type of dialect to be used will be derived from this if
484	         ``connection`` is not passed.
485	        :param dialect_name: string name of a dialect, such as
486	         "postgresql", "mssql", etc.
487	         The type of dialect to be used will be derived from this if
488	         ``connection`` and ``url`` are not passed.
489	        :param dialect_opts: dictionary of options to be passed to dialect
490	         constructor.
491	        :param transactional_ddl: Force the usage of "transactional"
492	         DDL on or off;
493	         this otherwise defaults to whether or not the dialect in
494	         use supports it.
495	        :param transaction_per_migration: if True, nest each migration script
496	         in a transaction rather than the full series of migrations to
497	         run.
498	        :param output_buffer: a file-like object that will be used
499	         for textual output
500	         when the ``--sql`` option is used to generate SQL scripts.
501	         Defaults to
502	         ``sys.stdout`` if not passed here and also not present on
503	         the :class:`.Config`
504	         object.  The value here overrides that of the :class:`.Config`
505	         object.
506	        :param output_encoding: when using ``--sql`` to generate SQL
507	         scripts, apply this encoding to the string output.
508	        :param literal_binds: when using ``--sql`` to generate SQL
509	         scripts, pass through the ``literal_binds`` flag to the compiler
510	         so that any literal values that would ordinarily be bound
511	         parameters are converted to plain strings.
512	
513	         .. warning:: Dialects can typically only handle simple datatypes
514	            like strings and numbers for auto-literal generation.  Datatypes
515	            like dates, intervals, and others may still require manual
516	            formatting, typically using :meth:`.Operations.inline_literal`.
517	
518	         .. note:: the ``literal_binds`` flag is ignored on SQLAlchemy
519	            versions prior to 0.8 where this feature is not supported.
520	
521	         .. seealso::
522	
523	            :meth:`.Operations.inline_literal`
524	
525	        :param starting_rev: Override the "starting revision" argument
526	         when using ``--sql`` mode.
527	        :param tag: a string tag for usage by custom ``env.py`` scripts.
528	         Set via the ``--tag`` option, can be overridden here.
529	        :param template_args: dictionary of template arguments which
530	         will be added to the template argument environment when
531	         running the "revision" command.   Note that the script environment
532	         is only run within the "revision" command if the --autogenerate
533	         option is used, or if the option "revision_environment=true"
534	         is present in the alembic.ini file.
535	
536	        :param version_table: The name of the Alembic version table.
537	         The default is ``'alembic_version'``.
538	        :param version_table_schema: Optional schema to place version
539	         table within.
540	        :param version_table_pk: boolean, whether the Alembic version table
541	         should use a primary key constraint for the "value" column; this
542	         only takes effect when the table is first created.
543	         Defaults to True; setting to False should not be necessary and is
544	         here for backwards compatibility reasons.
545	        :param on_version_apply: a callable or collection of callables to be
546	            run for each migration step.
547	            The callables will be run in the order they are given, once for
548	            each migration step, after the respective operation has been
549	            applied but before its transaction is finalized.
550	            Each callable accepts no positional arguments and the following
551	            keyword arguments:
552	
553	            * ``ctx``: the :class:`.MigrationContext` running the migration,
554	            * ``step``: a :class:`.MigrationInfo` representing the
555	              step currently being applied,
556	            * ``heads``: a collection of version strings representing the
557	              current heads,
558	            * ``run_args``: the ``**kwargs`` passed to :meth:`.run_migrations`.
559	
560	        Parameters specific to the autogenerate feature, when
561	        ``alembic revision`` is run with the ``--autogenerate`` feature:
562	
563	        :param target_metadata: a :class:`sqlalchemy.schema.MetaData`
564	         object, or a sequence of :class:`~sqlalchemy.schema.MetaData`
565	         objects, that will be consulted during autogeneration.
566	         The tables present in each :class:`~sqlalchemy.schema.MetaData`
567	         will be compared against
568	         what is locally available on the target
569	         :class:`~sqlalchemy.engine.Connection`
570	         to produce candidate upgrade/downgrade operations.
571	        :param compare_type: Indicates type comparison behavior during
572	         an autogenerate
573	         operation.  Defaults to ``True`` turning on type comparison, which
574	         has good accuracy on most backends.   See :ref:`compare_types`
575	         for an example as well as information on other type
576	         comparison options. Set to ``False`` which disables type
577	         comparison. A callable can also be passed to provide custom type
578	         comparison, see :ref:`compare_types` for additional details.
579	
580	         .. versionchanged:: 1.12.0 The default value of
581	            :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.compare_type` has been
582	            changed to ``True``.
583	
584	         .. seealso::
585	
586	            :ref:`compare_types`
587	
588	            :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.compare_server_default`
589	
590	        :param compare_server_default: Indicates server default comparison
591	         behavior during
592	         an autogenerate operation.  Defaults to ``False`` which disables
593	         server default
594	         comparison.  Set to  ``True`` to turn on server default comparison,
595	         which has
596	         varied accuracy depending on backend.
597	
598	         To customize server default comparison behavior, a callable may
599	         be specified
600	         which can filter server default comparisons during an
601	         autogenerate operation.
602	         defaults during an autogenerate operation.   The format of this
603	         callable is::
604	
605	            def my_compare_server_default(context, inspected_column,
606	                        metadata_column, inspected_default, metadata_default,
607	                        rendered_metadata_default):
608	                # return True if the defaults are different,
609	                # False if not, or None to allow the default implementation
610	                # to compare these defaults
611	                return None
612	
613	            context.configure(
614	                # ...
615	                compare_server_default = my_compare_server_default
616	            )
617	
618	         ``inspected_column`` is a dictionary structure as returned by
619	         :meth:`sqlalchemy.engine.reflection.Inspector.get_columns`, whereas
620	         ``metadata_column`` is a :class:`sqlalchemy.schema.Column` from
621	         the local model environment.
622	
623	         A return value of ``None`` indicates to allow default server default
624	         comparison
625	         to proceed.  Note that some backends such as Postgresql actually
626	         execute
627	         the two defaults on the database side to compare for equivalence.
628	
629	         .. seealso::
630	
631	            :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.compare_type`
632	
633	        :param include_name: A callable function which is given
634	         the chance to return ``True`` or ``False`` for any database reflected
635	         object based on its name, including database schema names when
636	         the :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.include_schemas` flag
637	         is set to ``True``.
638	
639	         The function accepts the following positional arguments:
640	
641	         * ``name``: the name of the object, such as schema name or table name.
642	           Will be ``None`` when indicating the default schema name of the
643	           database connection.
644	         * ``type``: a string describing the type of object; currently
645	           ``"schema"``, ``"table"``, ``"column"``, ``"index"``,
646	           ``"unique_constraint"``, or ``"foreign_key_constraint"``
647	         * ``parent_names``: a dictionary of "parent" object names, that are
648	           relative to the name being given.  Keys in this dictionary may
649	           include:  ``"schema_name"``, ``"table_name"`` or
650	           ``"schema_qualified_table_name"``.
651	
652	         E.g.::
653	
654	            def include_name(name, type_, parent_names):
655	                if type_ == "schema":
656	                    return name in ["schema_one", "schema_two"]
657	                else:
658	                    return True
659	
660	            context.configure(
661	                # ...
662	                include_schemas = True,
663	                include_name = include_name
664	            )
665	
666	         .. seealso::
667	
668	            :ref:`autogenerate_include_hooks`
669	
670	            :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.include_object`
671	
672	            :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.include_schemas`
673	
674	
675	        :param include_object: A callable function which is given
676	         the chance to return ``True`` or ``False`` for any object,
677	         indicating if the given object should be considered in the
678	         autogenerate sweep.
679	
680	         The function accepts the following positional arguments:
681	
682	         * ``object``: a :class:`~sqlalchemy.schema.SchemaItem` object such
683	           as a :class:`~sqlalchemy.schema.Table`,
684	           :class:`~sqlalchemy.schema.Column`,
685	           :class:`~sqlalchemy.schema.Index`
686	           :class:`~sqlalchemy.schema.UniqueConstraint`,
687	           or :class:`~sqlalchemy.schema.ForeignKeyConstraint` object
688	         * ``name``: the name of the object. This is typically available
689	           via ``object.name``.
690	         * ``type``: a string describing the type of object; currently
691	           ``"table"``, ``"column"``, ``"index"``, ``"unique_constraint"``,
692	           or ``"foreign_key_constraint"``
693	         * ``reflected``: ``True`` if the given object was produced based on
694	           table reflection, ``False`` if it's from a local :class:`.MetaData`
695	           object.
696	         * ``compare_to``: the object being compared against, if available,
697	           else ``None``.
698	
699	         E.g.::
700	
701	            def include_object(object, name, type_, reflected, compare_to):
702	                if (type_ == "column" and
703	                    not reflected and
704	                    object.info.get("skip_autogenerate", False)):
705	                    return False
706	                else:
707	                    return True
708	
709	            context.configure(
710	                # ...
711	                include_object = include_object
712	            )
713	
714	         For the use case of omitting specific schemas from a target database
715	         when :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.include_schemas` is
716	         set to ``True``, the :attr:`~sqlalchemy.schema.Table.schema`
717	         attribute can be checked for each :class:`~sqlalchemy.schema.Table`
718	         object passed to the hook, however it is much more efficient
719	         to filter on schemas before reflection of objects takes place
720	         using the :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.include_name`
721	         hook.
722	
723	         .. seealso::
724	
725	            :ref:`autogenerate_include_hooks`
726	
727	            :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.include_name`
728	
729	            :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.include_schemas`
730	
731	        :param render_as_batch: if True, commands which alter elements
732	         within a table will be placed under a ``with batch_alter_table():``
733	         directive, so that batch migrations will take place.
734	
735	         .. seealso::
736	
737	            :ref:`batch_migrations`
738	
739	        :param include_schemas: If True, autogenerate will scan across
740	         all schemas located by the SQLAlchemy
741	         :meth:`~sqlalchemy.engine.reflection.Inspector.get_schema_names`
742	         method, and include all differences in tables found across all
743	         those schemas.  When using this option, you may want to also
744	         use the :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.include_name`
745	         parameter to specify a callable which
746	         can filter the tables/schemas that get included.
747	
748	         .. seealso::
749	
750	            :ref:`autogenerate_include_hooks`
751	
752	            :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.include_name`
753	
754	            :paramref:`.EnvironmentContext.configure.include_object`
755	
756	        :param render_item: Callable that can be used to override how
757	         any schema item, i.e. column, constraint, type,
758	         etc., is rendered for autogenerate.  The callable receives a
759	         string describing the type of object, the object, and
760	         the autogen context.  If it returns False, the
761	         default rendering method will be used.  If it returns None,
762	         the item will not be rendered in the context of a Table
763	         construct, that is, can be used to skip columns or constraints
764	         within op.create_table()::
765	
766	            def my_render_column(type_, col, autogen_context):
767	                if type_ == "column" and isinstance(col, MySpecialCol):
768	                    return repr(col)
769	                else:
770	                    return False
771	
772	            context.configure(
773	                # ...
774	                render_item = my_render_column
775	            )
776	
777	         Available values for the type string include: ``"column"``,
778	         ``"primary_key"``, ``"foreign_key"``, ``"unique"``, ``"check"``,
779	         ``"type"``, ``"server_default"``.
780	
781	         .. seealso::
782	
783	            :ref:`autogen_render_types`
784	
785	        :param upgrade_token: When autogenerate completes, the text of the
786	         candidate upgrade operations will be present in this template
787	         variable when ``script.py.mako`` is rendered.  Defaults to
788	         ``upgrades``.
789	        :param downgrade_token: When autogenerate completes, the text of the
790	         candidate downgrade operations will be present in this
791	         template variable when ``script.py.mako`` is rendered.  Defaults to
792	         ``downgrades``.
793	
794	        :param alembic_module_prefix: When autogenerate refers to Alembic
795	         :mod:`alembic.operations` constructs, this prefix will be used
796	         (i.e. ``op.create_table``)  Defaults to "``op.``".
797	         Can be ``None`` to indicate no prefix.
798	
799	        :param sqlalchemy_module_prefix: When autogenerate refers to
800	         SQLAlchemy
801	         :class:`~sqlalchemy.schema.Column` or type classes, this prefix
802	         will be used
803	         (i.e. ``sa.Column("somename", sa.Integer)``)  Defaults to "``sa.``".
804	         Can be ``None`` to indicate no prefix.
805	         Note that when dialect-specific types are rendered, autogenerate
806	         will render them using the dialect module name, i.e. ``mssql.BIT()``,
807	         ``postgresql.UUID()``.
808	
809	        :param user_module_prefix: When autogenerate refers to a SQLAlchemy
810	         type (e.g. :class:`.TypeEngine`) where the module name is not
811	         under the ``sqlalchemy`` namespace, this prefix will be used
812	         within autogenerate.  If left at its default of
813	         ``None``, the ``__module__`` attribute of the type is used to
814	         render the import module.   It's a good practice to set this
815	         and to have all custom types be available from a fixed module space,
816	         in order to future-proof migration files against reorganizations
817	         in modules.
818	
819	         .. seealso::
820	
821	            :ref:`autogen_module_prefix`
822	
823	        :param process_revision_directives: a callable function that will
824	         be passed a structure representing the end result of an autogenerate
825	         or plain "revision" operation, which can be manipulated to affect
826	         how the ``alembic revision`` command ultimately outputs new
827	         revision scripts.   The structure of the callable is::
828	
829	            def process_revision_directives(context, revision, directives):
830	                pass
831	
832	         The ``directives`` parameter is a Python list containing
833	         a single :class:`.MigrationScript` directive, which represents
834	         the revision file to be generated.    This list as well as its
835	         contents may be freely modified to produce any set of commands.
836	         The section :ref:`customizing_revision` shows an example of
837	         doing this.  The ``context`` parameter is the
838	         :class:`.MigrationContext` in use,
839	         and ``revision`` is a tuple of revision identifiers representing the
840	         current revision of the database.
841	
842	         The callable is invoked at all times when the ``--autogenerate``
843	         option is passed to ``alembic revision``.  If ``--autogenerate``
844	         is not passed, the callable is invoked only if the
845	         ``revision_environment`` variable is set to True in the Alembic
846	         configuration, in which case the given ``directives`` collection
847	         will contain empty :class:`.UpgradeOps` and :class:`.DowngradeOps`
848	         collections for ``.upgrade_ops`` and ``.downgrade_ops``.  The
849	         ``--autogenerate`` option itself can be inferred by inspecting
850	         ``context.config.cmd_opts.autogenerate``.
851	
852	         The callable function may optionally be an instance of
853	         a :class:`.Rewriter` object.  This is a helper object that
854	         assists in the production of autogenerate-stream rewriter functions.
855	
856	         .. seealso::
857	
858	             :ref:`customizing_revision`
859	
860	             :ref:`autogen_rewriter`
861	
862	             :paramref:`.command.revision.process_revision_directives`
863	
864	        :param autogenerate_plugins: A list of string names of "plugins" that
865	         should participate in this autogenerate run.   Defaults to the list
866	         ``["alembic.autogenerate.*"]``, which indicates that Alembic's default
867	         autogeneration plugins will be used.
868	
869	         See the section :ref:`plugins_autogenerate` for complete background
870	         on how to use this parameter.
871	
872	         .. versionadded:: 1.18.0 Added a new plugin system for autogenerate
873	            compare directives.
874	
875	         .. seealso::
876	
877	            :ref:`plugins_autogenerate` - background on enabling/disabling
878	            autogenerate plugins
879	
880	            :ref:`alembic.plugins.toplevel` - Introduction and documentation
881	            to the plugin system
882	
883	        Parameters specific to individual backends:
884	
885	        :param mssql_batch_separator: The "batch separator" which will
886	         be placed between each statement when generating offline SQL Server
887	         migrations.  Defaults to ``GO``.  Note this is in addition to the
888	         customary semicolon ``;`` at the end of each statement; SQL Server
889	         considers the "batch separator" to denote the end of an
890	         individual statement execution, and cannot group certain
891	         dependent operations in one step.
892	        :param oracle_batch_separator: The "batch separator" which will
893	         be placed between each statement when generating offline
894	         Oracle migrations.  Defaults to ``/``.  Oracle doesn't add a
895	         semicolon between statements like most other backends.
896	
897	        """
898	        opts = self.context_opts
899	        if transactional_ddl is not None:
900	            opts["transactional_ddl"] = transactional_ddl
901	        if output_buffer is not None:
902	            opts["output_buffer"] = output_buffer
903	        elif self.config.output_buffer is not None:
904	            opts["output_buffer"] = self.config.output_buffer
905	        if starting_rev:
906	            opts["starting_rev"] = starting_rev
907	        if tag:
908	            opts["tag"] = tag
909	        if template_args and "template_args" in opts:
910	            opts["template_args"].update(template_args)
911	        opts["transaction_per_migration"] = transaction_per_migration
912	        opts["target_metadata"] = target_metadata
913	        opts["include_name"] = include_name
914	        opts["include_object"] = include_object
915	        opts["include_schemas"] = include_schemas
916	        opts["render_as_batch"] = render_as_batch
917	        opts["upgrade_token"] = upgrade_token
918	        opts["downgrade_token"] = downgrade_token
919	        opts["sqlalchemy_module_prefix"] = sqlalchemy_module_prefix
920	        opts["alembic_module_prefix"] = alembic_module_prefix
921	        opts["user_module_prefix"] = user_module_prefix
922	        opts["literal_binds"] = literal_binds
923	        opts["process_revision_directives"] = process_revision_directives
924	        opts["on_version_apply"] = util.to_tuple(on_version_apply, default=())
925	
926	        if autogenerate_plugins is not None:
927	            opts["autogenerate_plugins"] = autogenerate_plugins
928	
929	        if render_item is not None:
930	            opts["render_item"] = render_item
931	        opts["compare_type"] = compare_type
932	        if compare_server_default is not None:
933	            opts["compare_server_default"] = compare_server_default
934	        opts["script"] = self.script
935	
936	        opts.update(kw)
937	
938	        self._migration_context = MigrationContext.configure(
939	            connection=connection,
940	            url=url,
941	            dialect_name=dialect_name,
942	            environment_context=self,
943	            dialect_opts=dialect_opts,
944	            opts=opts,
945	        )
946

>> Issue: [B107:hardcoded_password_default] Possible hardcoded password: ''
   Severity: Low   Confidence: Medium
   CWE: CWE-259 (https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/259.html)
   More Info: https://bandit.readthedocs.io/en/1.9.4/plugins/b107_hardcoded_password_default.html
   Location: .\.venv\Lib\site-packages\openpyxl\utils\protection.py:4:0
3	
4	def hash_password(plaintext_password=''):
5	    """
6	    Create a password hash from a given string for protecting a worksheet
7	    only. This will not work for encrypting a workbook.
8	
9	    This method is based on the algorithm provided by
10	    Daniel Rentz of OpenOffice and the PEAR package
11	    Spreadsheet_Excel_Writer by Xavier Noguer <xnoguer@rezebra.com>.
12	    See also http://blogs.msdn.com/b/ericwhite/archive/2008/02/23/the-legacy-hashing-algorithm-in-open-xml.aspx
13	    """
14	    password = 0x0000
15	    for idx, char in enumerate(plaintext_password, 1):
16	        value = ord(char) << idx
17	        rotated_bits = value >> 15
18	        value &= 0x7fff
19	        password ^= (value | rotated_bits)
20	    password ^= len(plaintext_password)
21	    password ^= 0xCE4B
22	    return str(hex(password)).upper()[2:]
